questions.title: Convert Sorted Array to BST
questions.description: Convert sorted array to a height-balanced Binary Search Tree.
questions.explanation: Recursively selecting middle element as root ensures height-balanced BST construction.

questions.title: Copy List with Random Pointer
questions.description: Deep copy a linked list where each node has next and random pointer.
questions.explanation: Hash map O(n) space or interweaving O(1) space. Interweaving avoids extra space.
questions.code_solution:
def copyRandomList(head):
    if not head:
        return None
    curr = head
    while curr:
        new_node = Node(curr.val)
        new_node.next = curr.next
        curr.next = new_node
        curr = new_node.next
    curr = head
    while curr:
        if curr.random:
            curr.next.random = curr.random.next
        curr = curr.next.next
    old_list = head
    new_list = head.next
    result = head.next
    while old_list:
        old_list.next = old_list.next.next
        new_list.next = new_list.next.next if new_list.next else None
        old_list = old_list.next
        new_list = new_list.next
    return result

questions.title: Reorder List
questions.description: Reorder linked list L0→L1→...→Ln-1→Ln to L0→Ln→L1→Ln-1→L2→Ln-2→...
options: Find middle, reverse second half, merge
questions.explanation: Find middle + reverse + merge is optimal O(1) space. Stack uses O(n) space.
questions.code_solution:
def reorderList(head):
    if not head or not head.next:
        return
    slow = fast = head
    while fast and fast.next:
        slow = slow.next
        fast = fast.next.next
    prev, curr = None, slow.next
    slow.next = None
    while curr:
        nxt = curr.next
        curr.next = prev
        prev = curr
        curr = nxt
    first, second = head, prev
    while second:
        tmp1, tmp2 = first.next, second.next
        first.next = second
        second.next = tmp1
        first, second = tmp1, tmp2

questions.title: Balanced Binary Tree Check
questions.description: Check if binary tree is height-balanced (subtrees differ by at most 1).
questions.explanation: Bottom-up approach is optimal O(n). Top-down recalculates heights repeatedly.

questions.title: Construct Binary Tree from Preorder and Inorder

questions.explanation: Recursive with hashmap for O(1) index lookup is efficient and intuitive.

options: Hash maps + k-way merge with heap

questions.title: Insert Interval
questions.description: Insert new interval into sorted non-overlapping intervals and merge if needed.
questions.explanation: Linear scan is simple O(n). Binary search finds position faster but merging is still O(n).
questions.code_solution:
def insert(intervals, newInterval):
    result = []
    i = 0
    while i < len(intervals) and intervals[i][1] < newInterval[0]:
        result.append(intervals[i])
        i += 1
    while i < len(intervals) and intervals[i][0] <= newInterval[1]:
        newInterval[0] = min(newInterval[0], intervals[i][0])
        newInterval[1] = max(newInterval[1], intervals[i][1])
        i += 1
    result.append(newInterval)
    result.extend(intervals[i:])
    return result

questions.title: Course Schedule II (Topological Order)
questions.description: Return ordering of courses to finish all courses given prerequisites.
questions.explanation: Both Kahn's (BFS) and DFS produce valid topological ordering.
questions.code_solution:
def findOrder(numCourses, prerequisites):
    from collections import defaultdict, deque
    graph = defaultdict(list)
    indegree = [0] * numCourses
    for dest, src in prerequisites:
        graph[src].append(dest)
        indegree[dest] += 1
    queue = deque([i for i in range(numCourses) if indegree[i] == 0])
    order = []
    while queue:
        course = queue.popleft()
        order.append(course)
        for neighbor in graph[course]:
            indegree[neighbor] -= 1
            if indegree[neighbor] == 0:
                queue.append(neighbor)
    return order if len(order) == numCourses else []

questions.title: Walls and Gates Problem

questions.title: Maximum Subarray with One Deletion
questions.description: Find max subarray sum after deleting at most one element.
questions.explanation: Both DP states and forward-backward Kadane's work efficiently in O(n).
questions.code_solution:
def maximumSum(arr):
    if not arr:
        return 0
    no_del = arr[0]
    one_del = 0
    max_sum = arr[0]
    for i in range(1, len(arr)):
        one_del = max(no_del, one_del + arr[i])
        no_del = max(arr[i], no_del + arr[i])
        max_sum = max(max_sum, no_del, one_del)
    return max_sum

questions.title: Palindromic Substrings Count
questions.description: Count total number of palindromic substrings in a string.
questions.explanation: Expand around center is O(n²) with O(1) space. Manacher's is O(n).
questions.code_solution:
def countSubstrings(s):
    count = 0
    for center in range(len(s)):
        left = right = center
        while left >= 0 and right < len(s) and s[left] == s[right]:
            count += 1
            left -= 1
            right += 1
        left, right = center, center + 1
        while left >= 0 and right < len(s) and s[left] == s[right]:
            count += 1
            left -= 1
            right += 1
    return count

questions.title: Path Sum III
questions.description: Count paths in binary tree that sum to target (paths can start from any node).
options: DFS with prefix sum hashmap O(n)
questions.explanation: Prefix sum with hashmap is optimal O(n). From each node is O(n²).
questions.code_solution:
def pathSum(root, targetSum):
    from collections import defaultdict
    prefix = defaultdict(int)
    prefix[0] = 1
    def dfs(node, curr_sum):
        if not node:
            return 0
        curr_sum += node.val
        count = prefix[curr_sum - targetSum]
        prefix[curr_sum] += 1
        count += dfs(node.left, curr_sum)
        count += dfs(node.right, curr_sum)
        prefix[curr_sum] -= 1
        return count
    return dfs(root, 0)

questions.title: Swap Nodes in Pairs
questions.description: Swap every two adjacent nodes in linked list.
questions.explanation: Both iterative and recursive work. Iterative is more space-efficient.
questions.code_solution:
def swapPairs(head):
    dummy = ListNode(0)
    dummy.next = head
    prev = dummy
    while prev.next and prev.next.next:
        first = prev.next
        second = first.next
        first.next = second.next
        second.next = first
        prev.next = second
        prev = first
    return dummy.next

questions.title: Exclusive Time of Functions

questions.explanation: Stack tracks active functions. When new starts, pause current. On end, resume previous.

questions.title: Maximum Frequency Stack
questions.description: Design stack with push() and pop() removing most frequent element.
questions.sample_input: push(5), push(7), push(5), push(7), push(4), push(5), pop(), pop(), pop(), pop()
questions.explanation: HashMap mapping freq to stack of elements gives O(1) push and pop.
questions.code_solution:
class FreqStack:
    def __init__(self):
        from collections import defaultdict
        self.freq = defaultdict(int)
        self.group = defaultdict(list)
        self.max_freq = 0
    
    def push(self, val):
        self.freq[val] += 1
        f = self.freq[val]
        self.max_freq = max(self.max_freq, f)
        self.group[f].append(val)
    
    def pop(self):
        val = self.group[self.max_freq].pop()
        self.freq[val] -= 1
        if not self.group[self.max_freq]:
            self.max_freq -= 1
        return val

questions.title: Reorganize String
questions.description: Rearrange string so same characters are not adjacent. Return any possible.
options: Count and fill even then odd indices
questions.explanation: Heap approach is general. Fill even indices first works when max freq ≤ (n+1)/2.
questions.code_solution:
def reorganizeString(s):
    from collections import Counter
    import heapq
    count = Counter(s)
    heap = [(-freq, char) for char, freq in count.items()]
    heapq.heapify(heap)
    if -heap[0][0] > (len(s) + 1) // 2:
        return ""
    result = []
    prev = (0, '')
    while heap:
        freq, char = heapq.heappop(heap)
        result.append(char)
        if prev[0] < 0:
            heapq.heappush(heap, prev)
        prev = (freq + 1, char)
    return ''.join(result)

questions.title: String to Integer (atoi)
questions.description: Implement atoi which converts string to integer following specific rules.
questions.explanation: Sequential parsing following rules: skip whitespace, handle sign, accumulate digits.
questions.code_solution:
def myAtoi(s):
    s = s.strip()
    if not s:
        return 0
    sign = 1
    i = 0
    if s[0] in '+-':
        sign = -1 if s[0] == '-' else 1
        i = 1
    result = 0
    while i < len(s) and s[i].isdigit():
        digit = int(s[i])
        if result > 214748364 or (result == 214748364 and digit > 7):
            return 2147483647 if sign == 1 else -2147483648
        result = result * 10 + digit
        i += 1
    return sign * result

questions.title: Divide Two Integers
questions.description: Divide two integers without multiplication, division, or mod operator.
questions.explanation: Bit manipulation using left shifts for exponential subtraction is optimal O(logn).
questions.code_solution:
def divide(dividend, divisor):
    if dividend == -2147483648 and divisor == -1:
        return 2147483647
    sign = (dividend < 0) == (divisor < 0)
    dividend, divisor = abs(dividend), abs(divisor)
    result = 0
    while dividend >= divisor:
        temp, multiple = divisor, 1
        while dividend >= (temp << 1):
            temp <<= 1
            multiple <<= 1
        dividend -= temp
        result += multiple
    return result if sign else -result

questions.title: Ternary Expression Parser
questions.description: Parse ternary expression like T?2:3 or F?1:T?4:5.
options: Stack from right to left O(n)
questions.explanation: Process right-to-left using stack. When see '?', evaluate based on preceding char.
questions.code_solution:
def parseTernary(expression):
    stack = []
    i = len(expression) - 1
    while i >= 0:
        if expression[i].isdigit() or expression[i] in 'TF':
            stack.append(expression[i])
        elif expression[i] == '?':
            condition = expression[i-1]
            i -= 1
            true_val = stack.pop()
            false_val = stack.pop()
            stack.append(true_val if condition == 'T' else false_val)
        i -= 1
    return stack[0]

questions.title: Encode and Decode Strings
questions.description: Design algorithm to encode list of strings to single string and decode back.
questions.sample_output: Encoded then decoded back to original
questions.explanation: Length-prefixed (e.g., "5#Hello5#World") is robust against any characters.

questions.title: Smallest Range Covering Elements from K Lists
questions.description: Find smallest range that includes at least one number from each of k lists.
questions.explanation: Min-heap tracks smallest range covering all lists. Two-pointer on merged list also works.
questions.code_solution:
def smallestRange(nums):
    import heapq
    heap = []
    curr_max = float('-inf')
    for i, lst in enumerate(nums):
        heapq.heappush(heap, (lst[0], i, 0))
        curr_max = max(curr_max, lst[0])
    result = [heap[0][0], curr_max]
    while heap:
        val, list_idx, elem_idx = heapq.heappop(heap)
        if curr_max - val < result[1] - result[0]:
            result = [val, curr_max]
        if elem_idx + 1 == len(nums[list_idx]):
            break
        next_val = nums[list_idx][elem_idx + 1]
        heapq.heappush(heap, (next_val, list_idx, elem_idx + 1))
        curr_max = max(curr_max, next_val)
    return result

questions.title: Meeting Rooms (Check Overlap)
questions.description: Determine if a person can attend all meetings (no overlaps).
questions.explanation: Sort by start time. Check if any meeting starts before previous ends.

questions.title: Shortest Word Distance II
questions.description: Design structure for multiple shortest distance queries between two words.
questions.explanation: Store indices per word. Two-pointer merge is O(n). Binary search per query is O(logn).

questions.title: Plus One Linked List
questions.description: Add one to integer represented as linked list (MSB first).
questions.explanation: Finding rightmost non-9 digit avoids reversal. Stack also works.
questions.code_solution:
def plusOne(head):
    dummy = ListNode(0)
    dummy.next = head
    not_nine = dummy
    while head:
        if head.val != 9:
            not_nine = head
        head = head.next
    not_nine.val += 1
    not_nine = not_nine.next
    while not_nine:
        not_nine.val = 0
        not_nine = not_nine.next
    return dummy if dummy.val == 1 else dummy.next

questions.title: Maximum Average Subtree
questions.description: Find maximum average value among all subtrees of binary tree.
options: Compute average for each subtree separately
questions.explanation: Postorder returns (sum, count) for each subtree. Update global max average O(n).

questions.title: Graph Bipartite Check
questions.description: Check if graph can be divided into two sets with no edges within same set.
questions.explanation: BFS/DFS with two-color assignment is standard. Union-Find with 2*n nodes also works.

questions.title: Flip Equivalent Binary Trees
questions.description: Check if two binary trees are flip equivalent (can swap children).
questions.explanation: Recursively check if children match directly or flipped. O(min(n1,n2)) time.

questions.title: Number of Matching Subsequences
questions.description: Count words in list that are subsequences of string S.
questions.explanation: Preprocess next char positions. For each word, check using pointer jumps.

questions.title: Binary Tree Preorder Iterator
questions.description: Implement BSTIterator that does preorder traversal (root, left, right).
options: Stack push right then left O(h) space
questions.explanation: Stack pushing right child then left child achieves preorder iteration.

questions.title: Delete Nodes and Return Forest
questions.description: Delete given nodes from binary tree and return roots of remaining forest.
options: Postorder DFS with parent tracking O(n)
questions.explanation: DFS returning whether node is root. If parent deleted but node not, add to result.
questions.code_solution:
def delNodes(root, to_delete):
    to_delete = set(to_delete)
    result = []
    def helper(node, is_root):
        if not node:
            return None
        deleted = node.val in to_delete
        if is_root and not deleted:
            result.append(node)
        node.left = helper(node.left, deleted)
        node.right = helper(node.right, deleted)
        return None if deleted else node
    helper(root, True)
    return result

DFS tracking max depth and sum O(n)

questions.title: Maximum Difference Between Node and Ancestor

questions.explanation: DFS tracking current min and max along path. At each node compute max diff.

questions.title: Count Good Nodes in Binary Tree

options: DFS tracking max so far O(n)
questions.explanation: DFS maintaining current maximum along path. Node is 'good' if val >= max_so_far.

questions.title: Minimum Flips to Make a OR b Equal to c
questions.description: Find min flips in bits of a and b to make (a OR b) == c.
questions.explanation: For each bit: if c=0, need a=0,b=0 (flips = a_bit + b_bit). If c=1, need at least one=1.

questions.title: Maximum XOR of Two Numbers in Array

questions.explanation: Trie approach is O(32n). HashSet with greedy bit building also works.

questions.title: Non-overlapping Intervals

options: Sort by end time, keep non-overlapping O(nlogn)
Sort by start time, DP O(n²)
questions.explanation: Classic interval scheduling: sort by end time, greedily keep earliest finishing valid intervals.

questions.title: Find the Town Judge

questions.explanation: Track trust counts: indegree - outdegree. Judge has n-1 indegree and 0 outdegree.

questions.title: Minimum Domino Rotations

questions.explanation: Only 4 candidates (a[0] in top/bottom, b[0] in top/bottom). Count rotations needed.

questions.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock II
questions.description: Max profit with multiple transactions (buy one, sell one at a time).
questions.explanation: Greedy: add profit whenever price rises. Equivalent to capturing every upward slope.

questions.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock with Cooldown

questions.explanation: States: held, sold (cooldown), rest. Track max profit at each state for each day.

questions.title: Minimum Cost to Connect Sticks
questions.description: Connect sticks with cost equal to sum of lengths. Minimize total cost.
questions.explanation: Always combine two smallest sticks. Min-heap simulates this efficiently.
questions.code_solution:
def connectSticks(sticks):
    import heapq
    heapq.heapify(sticks)
    cost = 0
    while len(sticks) > 1:
        a = heapq.heappop(sticks)
        b = heapq.heappop(sticks)
        combined = a + b
        cost += combined
        heapq.heappush(sticks, combined)
    return cost

questions.title: Maximum Product of Splitted Binary Tree
questions.description: Split tree by removing one edge. Maximize product of two subtree sums.
options: Postorder total sum then check O(n)
questions.explanation: First pass: total sum. Second pass: for each subtree sum s, product = s * (total - s).
questions.code_solution:
def maxProduct(root):
    total = 0
    def sum_tree(node):
        nonlocal total
        if not node:
            return 0
        total += node.val
        s = node.val + sum_tree(node.left) + sum_tree(node.right)
        return s
    total += root.val + sum_tree(root.left) + sum_tree(root.right)
    max_prod = 0
    def dfs(node):
        nonlocal max_prod
        if not node:
            return 0
        left = dfs(node.left)
        right = dfs(node.right)
        subtree_sum = node.val + left + right
        max_prod = max(max_prod, subtree_sum * (total - subtree_sum))
        return subtree_sum
    dfs(root)
    return max_prod % (10**9 + 7)

questions.title: All Nodes Distance K in Binary Tree
questions.description: Return all nodes at distance K from target node in binary tree.
questions.explanation: Build parent pointers, then BFS from target. Two-phase DFS also works.

questions.title: Shortest Distance from All Buildings
questions.description: Find empty land with smallest total distance to all buildings.
questions.explanation: BFS from each building accumulating distances. Check reachability from all buildings.

questions.title: Binary Tree Coloring Game
questions.description: Two players pick nodes. Second player picks after first. Can second win?
questions.explanation: Second player can win by blocking first player's parent, left, or right if that subtree > n/2.
questions.code_solution:
def btreeGameWinningMove(root, n, x):
    left_count = [0]
    right_count = [0]
    def count(node):
        if not node:
            return 0
        l = count(node.left)
        r = count(node.right)
        if node.val == x:
            left_count[0] = l
            right_count[0] = r
        return l + r + 1
    count(root)
    parent_count = n - left_count[0] - right_count[0] - 1
    return max(parent_count, left_count[0], right_count[0]) > n // 2

questions.title: Minimum Knight Moves

questions.title: Reconstruct Itinerary
questions.description: Reconstruct flight itinerary starting from JFK using all tickets once.
options: Greedy with heap at each airport
questions.explanation: Hierholzer's finds Eulerian path in O(ElogE). DFS with heap for lexical order.
questions.code_solution:
def findItinerary(tickets):
    from collections import defaultdict
    import heapq
    graph = defaultdict(list)
    for src, dst in tickets:
        heapq.heappush(graph[src], dst)
    itinerary = []
    def dfs(airport):
        while graph[airport]:
            next_airport = heapq.heappop(graph[airport])
            dfs(next_airport)
        itinerary.append(airport)
    dfs('JFK')
    return itinerary[::-1]

questions.title: Evaluate Division
questions.description: Answer division queries based on given equations and values.

questions.title: Sliding Window Median

questions.title: Data Stream as Disjoint Intervals

questions.explanation: BST/TreeMap stores intervals. On adding number, merge adjacent intervals if exists.

questions.title: Count of Smaller Numbers After Self
questions.description: For each element, count number of smaller elements to its right.
questions.explanation: Merge sort counting inversions is elegant. BIT and BST also work.
questions.code_solution:
def countSmaller(nums):
    import bisect
    sorted_list = []
    result = []
    for num in reversed(nums):
        i = bisect.bisect_left(sorted_list, num)
        result.append(i)
        sorted_list.insert(i, num)
    return result[::-1]

questions.title: Number of Subarrays with Bounded Maximum

questions.title: Minimum Area Rectangle

questions.title: Number of Submatrices That Sum to Target

options: Prefix sum + hashmap per row pair O(m²n)
questions.explanation: Fix two rows, compute column prefix sums, use 1D subarray sum equals k technique.

questions.title: Maximal Square
questions.description: Find largest square containing only 1's in binary matrix.
questions.explanation: DP: dp[i][j] = min(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1], dp[i-1][j-1]) + 1 if matrix[i][j]=='1'.

questions.title: Surrounded Regions
questions.description: Capture all 'O' regions that are fully surrounded by 'X' on board.
questions.explanation: Mark all O's connected to border as safe (T). Then convert remaining O's to X.

questions.title: Cherry Pickup
questions.description: Two trips top-left to bottom-right and back. Collect cherries. Maximize total.
options: Both A and C are correct
questions.explanation: Equivalent to two people walking from top-left to bottom-right simultaneously. 3D DP.

questions.title: Critical Connections in Network
questions.description: Find all edges (bridges) in network that would disconnect graph if removed.
options: Naive: remove each edge and check O(E*(V+E))
questions.explanation: Tarjan's algorithm or DFS tracking discovery and low times finds bridges in O(V+E).
questions.code_solution:
def criticalConnections(n, connections):
    from collections import defaultdict
    graph = defaultdict(list)
    for u, v in connections:
        graph[u].append(v)
        graph[v].append(u)
    disc = [-1] * n
    low = [-1] * n
    result = []
    time = 0
    def dfs(node, parent):
        nonlocal time
        disc[node] = low[node] = time
        time += 1
        for neighbor in graph[node]:
            if disc[neighbor] == -1:
                dfs(neighbor, node)
                low[node] = min(low[node], low[neighbor])
                if low[neighbor] > disc[node]:
                    result.append([node, neighbor])
            elif neighbor != parent:
                low[node] = min(low[node], disc[neighbor])
    dfs(0, -1)
    return result

options: Bucket sort with width t+1 O(n)

options: DP tracking last two positions O(n)
questions.explanation: For each char, count substrings where it appears exactly once using last two occurrences.

questions.title: Range Module
questions.description: Design data structure tracking set of intervals with add, remove, query.
questions.explanation: TreeMap maintains disjoint intervals. Binary search for insertion/removal points.

questions.title: Shortest Path Visiting All Nodes
questions.description: Find shortest path length visiting all nodes in connected graph.
questions.explanation: BFS on state (node, mask) finds shortest path. DP also solves Traveling Salesman variant.

questions.title: Number of Longest Increasing Subsequence

options: DP O(n²) with lengths and counts
questions.explanation: DP: len[i] = max LIS ending at i. count[i] = sum of counts of predecessors.

questions.title: Russian Doll Envelopes

questions.title: Minimum Cost to Hire K Workers
questions.description: Hire exactly k workers paying wage proportional to quality. Minimize cost.
questions.explanation: Sort workers by wage/quality ratio. Maintain k workers with min quality sum using max-heap.
questions.code_solution:
def mincostToHireWorkers(quality, wage, k):
    import heapq
    workers = sorted([(w/q, q, w) for q, w in zip(quality, wage)])
    heap = []
    quality_sum = 0
    min_cost = float('inf')
    for ratio, q, w in workers:
        heapq.heappush(heap, -q)
        quality_sum += q
        if len(heap) > k:
            quality_sum += heapq.heappop(heap)
        if len(heap) == k:
            min_cost = min(min_cost, ratio * quality_sum)
    return min_cost

questions.title: IP to CIDR
questions.description: Convert IP address range to CIDR blocks covering exactly n addresses.
questions.explanation: Find largest block size (power of 2) that fits in remaining addresses starting from current IP.

questions.title: Find Median from Data Stream

questions.title: Perfect Rectangle

questions.title: Cut Off Trees for Golf Event

options: Sort trees + BFS between each O(mn * #trees)

questions.title: Strange Printer

questions.title: Swim in Rising Water
questions.description: Find minimum time to reach bottom-right in grid where water rises t units per time.
questions.explanation: Binary search on time T, check if path exists with values ≤ T. Dijkstra finds min max path.
questions.code_solution:
def swimInWater(grid):
    import heapq
    n = len(grid)
    visited = [[False] * n for _ in range(n)]
    heap = [(grid[0][0], 0, 0)]
    visited[0][0] = True
    result = 0
    directions = [(0,1), (0,-1), (1,0), (-1,0)]
    while heap:
        val, i, j = heapq.heappop(heap)
        result = max(result, val)
        if i == n-1 and j == n-1:
            return result
        for di, dj in directions:
            ni, nj = i + di, j + dj
            if 0 <= ni < n and 0 <= nj < n and not visited[ni][nj]:
                visited[ni][nj] = True
                heapq.heappush(heap, (grid[ni][nj], ni, nj))

questions.title: Employee Free Time
questions.description: Find common free time intervals across all employee schedules.
options: Min-heap of next interval per employee
questions.explanation: Collect all intervals, sort by start, merge. Free time is gaps between merged intervals.

questions.title: Palindrome Removal
questions.description: Minimum moves to remove all elements. Can remove palindromic subarray in one move.
questions.explanation: DP on intervals: dp[i][j] = min moves to remove arr[i..j]. Check palindromic properties.

questions.title: The Maze III
questions.description: Ball rolls in maze until hitting wall. Find path with smallest distance (lexicographically smallest).
questions.explanation: Dijkstra tracking distance and path string. Compare lexicographically when distances equal.

questions.title: Distinct Subsequences
questions.description: Count distinct subsequences of s that equal t.
questions.explanation: DP[i][j] = number of ways to form t[0..j] from s[0..i]. Handle matching/non-matching.

questions.title: Shortest Distance to Target Color
questions.description: For each query[i], find shortest distance to target color in array.
questions.explanation: Precompute nearest color index from left and right for each position. O(n) preprocessing.

questions.title: Largest Component Size by Common Factor
questions.description: Find largest connected component where nodes share common factor > 1.
questions.explanation: Factorize each number, union numbers sharing common prime factor using Union-Find.

questions.title: Grid Illumination
questions.description: Lamps illuminate row, column, both diagonals. Answer queries turning off lamps in adjacent 3x3.
questions.explanation: Track counts of lamps per row, column, diag, anti-diag. Check if any count > 0.

questions.title: Transform to Chessboard
questions.description: Find minimum swaps to transform binary matrix into chessboard pattern.
questions.explanation: Check board properties. Count misplacements for both chessboard patterns.

questions.title: Constrained Subsequence Sum
questions.description: Find max sum of subsequence where adjacent elements differ by at most k indices.
questions.explanation: DP[i] = nums[i] + max(0, max(dp[i-k..i-1])). Deque maintains max in sliding window.

questions.title: Maximum Profit in Job Scheduling
questions.description: Schedule non-overlapping jobs to maximize profit (weighted interval scheduling).
questions.explanation: Sort by end time. DP[i] = max profit ending at or before job i. Binary search for last non-conflicting.
questions.code_solution:
def jobScheduling(startTime, endTime, profit):
    jobs = sorted(zip(endTime, startTime, profit))
    end_times = [job[0] for job in jobs]
    dp = [0] * (len(jobs) + 1)
    import bisect
    for i, (end, start, p) in enumerate(jobs):
        j = bisect.bisect_right(end_times, start, 0, i)
        dp[i+1] = max(dp[i], dp[j] + p)
    return dp[-1]

questions.title: Word Squares
questions.description: Given word list, find all word squares (kth row and col read same).
questions.explanation: Build word square row by row. Use prefix hashmap or Trie to find words matching column prefixes.

questions.title: Race Car
questions.description: Car starts at position 0, speed 1. A=accelerate(pos+=speed,speed*=2), R=reverse(speed=±1). Find min instructions to reach target.
questions.explanation: BFS on (position, speed) state. Prune when position too far from target.

questions.title: Sum of Distances in Tree
questions.description: Find sum of distances from each node to all other nodes in tree.
questions.explanation: First DFS: compute count and distance sum for subtrees. Second DFS: reroot to compute for all nodes.

questions.title: Valid Palindrome III
questions.description: Check if string can become palindrome by deleting at most k characters.
questions.explanation: Min deletions to make palindrome = n - LCS(s, reverse(s)). Check if n - LPS ≤ k.

questions.title: Design Excel Sum Formula
questions.description: Design Excel with cells supporting values and sum formulas from rectangular ranges.
questions.explanation: Graph: cells depend on other cells. On set, update value. On sum, evaluate dependencies.

questions.title: String Transforms Into Another String
questions.description: Check if str1 can transform to str2 by repeatedly converting all same chars to another.
questions.explanation: Map characters from str1 to str2. Must be bijection. Additional check for unused intermediate character.

questions.title: Minimum Number of Refueling Stops
questions.description: Car starts with startFuel. Along route are stations with fuel[i]. Find min stops.
questions.explanation: Drive as far as possible. When empty, refuel from max fuel among passed stations using heap.
questions.code_solution:
def minRefuelStops(target, startFuel, stations):
    import heapq
    heap = []
    fuel = startFuel
    stops = 0
    prev = 0
    for pos, gas in stations + [(target, 0)]:
        fuel -= (pos - prev)
        while heap and fuel < 0:
            fuel += -heapq.heappop(heap)
            stops += 1
        if fuel < 0:
            return -1
        heapq.heappush(heap, -gas)
        prev = pos
    return stops

questions.title: Binary Tree Cameras
questions.description: Place cameras in binary tree. Each camera monitors itself, parent, and children. Minimize cameras.
options: DP with 3 states per node O(n)
questions.explanation: Postorder: each node returns state (0=uncovered, 1=camera, 2=covered). Bottom-up placement.

questions.title: Longest Duplicate Substring
questions.description: Find longest duplicate substring (repeated at least twice) in string.
questions.explanation: Binary search on length. For each length, check for duplicates using rolling hash (Rabin-Karp).

questions.title: Profitable Schemes
questions.description: Find number of schemes to achieve at least minProfit with n members committing crimes.
questions.explanation: DP[i][j][k] = ways using first i crimes, j members, k profit. Compress profit dimension.

questions.title: Hand of Straights
questions.description: Check if array can be divided into groups of W consecutive cards.
questions.explanation: Count cards. Start from smallest each time, try to form group of W consecutive cards.

questions.title: Array of Doubled Pairs
questions.description: Check if array can be reordered such that for every pair, second = 2 * first.
questions.explanation: Sort by absolute value. For each, check if its double exists. Handle negatives carefully.

questions.title: Video Stitching
questions.description: Find min clips to cover entire range [0, time]. Can cut clips.
questions.explanation: Similar to jump game: sort by start, greedily extend coverage with clip having max end time.
questions.code_solution:
def videoStitching(clips, time):
    clips.sort()
    curr_end = 0
    clips_used = 0
    i = 0
    while curr_end < time:
        max_end = curr_end
        while i < len(clips) and clips[i][0] <= curr_end:
            max_end = max(max_end, clips[i][1])
            i += 1
        if max_end == curr_end:
            return -1
        curr_end = max_end
        clips_used += 1
    return clips_used

questions.title: Integer Replacement
questions.description: Min operations to replace n with 1: if even n/=2, if odd n+1 or n-1.
questions.explanation: For even: n/2. For odd (except 3): if (n+1)%4==0, increment; else decrement.

questions.title: Minimum Genetic Mutation
questions.description: Find minimum mutations to transform start gene to end gene using valid bank.
questions.explanation: BFS on gene space. For each gene, try mutating each position to other 3 chars.

questions.title: Freedom Trail
questions.description: Rotate ring to spell key by aligning characters. Find minimum rotation steps.
questions.explanation: DP[i][j] = min steps to spell key[i:] being at ring position j. Check both rotation directions.

questions.title: Parse Lisp Expression
questions.description: Evaluate Lisp-like expressions with let, add, mult operations.
questions.sample_input: expression = "(let x 2 (mult x (let x 3 y 4 (add x y))))"
questions.explanation: Recursive parsing handling nested scopes. Tokenize and evaluate recursively.

questions.title: The Skyline Problem
questions.description: Given buildings, compute skyline (key points where height changes).
questions.explanation: Divide and conquer merges skylines. Line sweep uses heap to track current max height.

questions.title: Regular Expression Matching
questions.description: Implement regex matching with '.' and '*' supporting wildcards.
questions.explanation: DP: dp[i][j] matches s[0..i] with pattern p[0..j]. Handle * by zero or more preceding element.

questions.title: Optimal Account Balancing
questions.description: Find minimum transactions to settle debts among group.
questions.explanation: Calculate net balance. Use DFS to settle debts. DP with bitmask for optimal solution.

questions.title: Longest Absolute File Path
questions.description: Find length of longest absolute path to file in filesystem represented as string.
questions.explanation: Use stack to store cumulative path length at each depth. Detect file by '.' in name.

questions.title: Find the Celebrity
questions.description: Find celebrity (known by all, knows nobody) using minimum knows(a,b) calls.
questions.explanation: Find candidate by elimination: if knows(a,b), a is not celebrity else b is not. Then verify.

questions.title: K Empty Slots
questions.description: Flowers bloom one per day. Find day when k empty slots between two blooming flowers.
questions.explanation: Track positions. For each new bloom, check distance to neighbors. Sliding window approach: check k+2 sized window.

questions.title: Minimum Time to Build Blocks
questions.description: Build all blocks. Can split worker (cost=split) or have worker build block (cost=block[i]).
questions.explanation: Similar to Huffman coding. Always combine two smallest blocks. Equivalent to building tree.
questions.code_solution:
def minBuildTime(blocks, split):
    import heapq
    heapq.heapify(blocks)
    while len(blocks) > 1:
        a = heapq.heappop(blocks)
        b = heapq.heappop(blocks)
        heapq.heappush(blocks, split + max(a, b))
    return blocks[0]

questions.title: Binary Searchable Elements
questions.description: Count elements that can be found by binary search in unsorted array.
options: Precompute left max and right min O(n)
questions.explanation: Element is binary searchable if all elements to left are smaller and all to right are larger.
questions.code_solution:
def binarySearchable(nums):
    n = len(nums)
    left_max = [float('-inf')] * n
    right_min = [float('inf')] * n
    curr_max = float('-inf')
    for i in range(n):
        curr_max = max(curr_max, nums[i])
        left_max[i] = curr_max
    curr_min = float('inf')
    for i in range(n-1, -1, -1):
        curr_min = min(curr_min, nums[i])
        right_min[i] = curr_min
    count = 0
    for i in range(n):
        if left_max[i] <= nums[i] <= right_min[i]:
            count += 1
    return count

questions.title: Snapshot Array
questions.description: Design array supporting snapshots and value retrieval at specific snap_id.
options: HashMap per index O(logS) per query
questions.explanation: Each index stores list of (snap_id, value). Binary search to find value at given snap_id.

questions.title: Bulls and Cows
questions.description: Guess number game: count bulls (correct position) and cows (wrong position, correct digit).
questions.explanation: First pass count bulls. Second pass count cows using frequency array for remaining digits.

questions.title: Sentence Screen Fitting
questions.description: Find how many times sentence can fit on screen with given rows and cols.
options: DP with next index O(rows + n)
questions.explanation: For each word as row start, compute next row's start word and count. Detect cycles.

questions.title: Moving Average from Data Stream
questions.description: Calculate moving average of last k elements in stream.
questions.explanation: Queue maintains last k elements. Update running sum on add/remove. O(1) next().

questions.title: Kth Smallest Instructions
questions.description: Find kth lexicographically smallest path from [0,0] to [row,col] using H and V moves.
options: Choose next move based on remaining combinations
questions.explanation: At each step, if remaining combinations with H ≥ k, choose H; else choose V and reduce k.

questions.title: Find the Most Competitive Subsequence
questions.description: Find most competitive subsequence of size k (smallest lexicographically).
questions.explanation: Monotonic increasing stack maintaining at most k elements. Pop if larger and enough remaining.

questions.title: Flower Planting With No Adjacent
questions.description: Color garden nodes (1-4) such that no adjacent nodes have same color.
questions.explanation: Since max degree ≤ 3 and colors = 4, greedy coloring always works. Assign any available color.

questions.title: Check If Word Is Valid After Substitutions
questions.description: Check if string can be formed by repeatedly inserting "abc" at any position.
questions.explanation: Stack: push characters. If top 3 form 'abc', pop them. Check if stack empty at end.

questions.title: Count Triplets That Can Form Two Arrays of Equal XOR
questions.description: Count triplets (i,j,k) where XOR(arr[i..j-1]) == XOR(arr[j..k]).
questions.explanation: Condition equivalent to XOR(arr[i..k]) == 0. Then any j between i+1 and k works.
